_calibration_note: All numeric values below are initial estimates. Re-tune against a human-scored scene once real footage data arrives. See docs/PROGRESS.md 'Known gaps' for the calibration backlog.

trust_levels._note: Scale 0–1. Higher = more weight when resolving expected vs observed. Human-confirmed facts short-circuit to 1.0 regardless.

category_weights._note: Relative penalty multiplier per category. Increase to make that category matter more to the overall score. Validate against audience/producer sensitivity once screening-level data exists.

severity_penalties._note: Points subtracted from 100 per issue at this severity (before category weight and confidence scaling). Baseline: low=10, medium=30, high=50, critical=75. Adjust downward if the score feels too punitive on noisy footage.

thresholds._note: Raise min_observation_confidence to reduce noise from low-quality vision detections. Lower entity_match_similarity if aliases are not catching all name variations.

assumptions._note: default_expires_after_scenes: how many scenes after a disturbance its softening effect persists. max_penalty_reduction: max fraction of penalty that assumptions can absorb (0.7 = up to 70% softening).

repetition._note: After escalate_after_occurrences of the same issue type on the same entity, severity is bumped up one level (capped at max_severity).

attribute_aliases._note: Maps canonical attribute names to alternate field names that Team 1/2 may use. Add new entries here instead of changing parser code.

value_synonyms._note: Groups of values treated as equivalent during comparison. Add production-specific synonyms here (e.g. character costume variants across scenes).
